product_principle: Calendar should become the meeting source of truth while Minutia becomes the system of record for A/D/R/I/B, ownership, and follow-through.

north_star.definition: Percentage of attended calendar meetings where the user can start or join Minutia capture from the sidebar without creating a series or meeting manually.

implemented: Google Workspace OAuth scopes are split across identity, calendar readonly, and directory readonly.
Calendar sidebar can fetch agenda events and open event details.
Recurring Google Calendar entries map to recurring Minutia series through recurringEventId and originalStartTime.
Ad hoc Google Calendar entries map to one-off Minutia series and meetings.
Users can still create Minutia-only series outside Google Calendar.
Starting a calendar event can open the meeting URL and route to Minutia live capture.
Synced live meetings now show Meeting in progress and Join now instead of Start meeting.
Workspace Directory search can power assignee suggestions for issue ownership.
known_gaps: Agenda sync is request-driven from the sidebar instead of webhook plus incremental sync.
Calendar event lifecycle changes are not yet reflected proactively when the user is not viewing the sidebar.
OAuth health, partial scope loss, and sync failure recovery need clearer product states.
Google Meet transcript import is not implemented.
Official real-time Meet media access is Developer Preview, so live A/D/R/I/B automation needs a gated research track.

capability_map.product_use: Populate the right sidebar agenda and create linked Minutia meetings.
notes: events.list supports singleEvents=true and orderBy=startTime for expanded instances.
Recurring instances expose recurringEventId and originalStartTime, which are enough for stable series and instance identity.

capability_map.product_use: Keep Minutia state fresh without repeated full agenda scans.
notes: Initial full sync returns nextSyncToken on the final page.
Incremental sync uses syncToken and must handle deleted entries.

capability_map.product_use: Wake sync when events change and update sidebar state quickly.
notes: Push notifications are per calendar for Events resources.
Notifications contain no event payload, so Minutia must call Calendar again after receiving a webhook.
Notifications are not guaranteed to be 100 percent reliable, so periodic reconciliation is still required.

capability_map.product_use: Suggest assignees from company directory when assigning issues, risks, blockers, or actions.
notes: Supports query, readMask, sources, pageSize, and pageToken.

capability_map.product_use: Future scheduling helpers, prep nudges, and conflict warnings.
notes: calendar.readonly is sufficient for freebusy query in the current source documentation.

capability_map.product_use: Import transcript entries after the meeting and auto-suggest A/D/R/I/B items.
notes: Participants must configure transcripts before the conference ends.
Meet transcript entries are available for a limited retention window.
This is better for post-meeting automation than live capture.

capability_map.product_use: Research path for live A/D/R/I/B extraction during the call.
notes: The Google Cloud project, OAuth principal, and all conference participants must be enrolled in the Google Workspace Developer Preview Program.
This should be gated behind explicit consent and admin controls.

product_decisions.decision: Calendar meetings are suggestions until the user starts or joins capture.
product_decisions.reason: This avoids surprising users by creating live capture state for every invite while still removing setup friction.

product_decisions.decision: Recurring calendar series create one Minutia series, while each occurrence creates one meeting.
product_decisions.reason: Users think about recurring meetings as one operating cadence with many meeting instances.

product_decisions.decision: Ad hoc calendar events create ad hoc Minutia series by default.
product_decisions.reason: The existing Minutia data model expects a meeting to belong to a series, so ad hoc series preserve model consistency without hiding the calendar origin.
Calendar should reduce setup, not become a hard dependency for self-hosted or offline workflows.

product_decisions.decision: Live meeting state comes from Minutia, not only Google Calendar.
product_decisions.reason: Once capture starts, the authoritative user experience is the Minutia meeting lifecycle.

product_decisions.decision: Transcript automation starts with post-meeting import, while live transcription remains research-gated.
product_decisions.reason: Google Meet REST artifacts support transcripts after generation, while real-time Meet media access is Developer Preview.

roadmap.name: Trust and correctness
roadmap.outcome: Users can connect Google Workspace, see calendar meetings, and avoid duplicate live meetings.

roadmap.name: Reliable calendar sync
roadmap.outcome: Calendar changes flow into Minutia reliably, with visible sync health and repair actions.

roadmap.name: Meeting intelligence
roadmap.outcome: Minutia uses calendar context, attendees, and history to make meetings easier to run.

roadmap.name: Transcript automation
roadmap.outcome: A/D/R/I/B can be suggested from official Meet artifacts after meetings, with live extraction gated by preview access and consent.

use_cases.name: Connect Google Workspace
use_cases.trigger: The user connects Google from Settings.
use_cases.success_outcome: Minutia stores encrypted Google tokens and can read calendar events and directory users based on granted scopes.
use_cases.failure_outcome: Minutia shows exactly which capability is missing, such as calendar access or directory access.

use_cases.name: Review Today Agenda
use_cases.trigger: The user opens the right calendar sidebar.
use_cases.success_outcome: The sidebar shows the user's calendar meetings for the selected day with linked Minutia series, meeting status, attendees, and meeting URL.

use_cases.name: Create Recurring Series From Calendar
use_cases.trigger: A recurring calendar event appears in the agenda.
use_cases.success_outcome: Minutia creates or updates one series for the recurring calendar identity and one meeting for the occurrence.

use_cases.name: Create Ad Hoc Meeting From Calendar
use_cases.trigger: A one-off calendar event appears in the agenda.
use_cases.success_outcome: Minutia creates or updates an ad hoc series and meeting without forcing the user through manual series setup.

use_cases.name: Start or Join Synced Meeting
use_cases.trigger: The user opens a calendar event detail and presses the primary action.
use_cases.success_outcome: Minutia opens the meeting URL in a new tab when available and opens live capture in the current Minutia tab.

use_cases.name: Assign Work Using Directory
use_cases.trigger: The user assigns an action, risk, blocker, issue, or decision owner.
use_cases.success_outcome: Assignee search returns Workspace Directory matches so ownership can be assigned to the correct colleague.

use_cases.name: Recover From Calendar Drift
use_cases.trigger: A calendar event is moved, cancelled, rescheduled, or attendee list changes.
use_cases.success_outcome: Minutia updates linked records, preserves captured meeting history, and explains any destructive or ambiguous change.

use_cases.name: Import Meet Transcript
use_cases.trigger: A Google Meet transcript artifact is generated after the meeting.
use_cases.success_outcome: Minutia imports transcript entries and suggests A/D/R/I/B items for human approval.

use_cases.name: Run AI-Native Calendar Agent
use_cases.trigger: An agent needs to inspect upcoming meetings, prep gaps, or follow-up obligations.
use_cases.success_outcome: Minutia exposes scriptable calendar-linked data through stable APIs or MCP-compatible surfaces without browser scraping.

user_stories.title: Connect Google Workspace
user_stories.user_story: As a workspace member, I want to connect Google Workspace once so Minutia can read my calendar meetings and company directory.
user_stories.product_decision: Use one clear Google Workspace connection surface, but report calendar and directory capability separately after OAuth.
acceptance_criteria: Settings shows Google Workspace connection status.
Connected status includes whether Calendar and Directory access are available.
Disconnect revokes Google access and removes stored tokens.
Tokens are encrypted at rest and never exposed to the browser.
test_strategy: OAuth regression covers scope handling and callback redirect.
Settings regression covers connected, disconnected, and partial-scope messaging.

user_stories.title: Auto-populate right sidebar agenda
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting participant, I want my calendar meetings to appear in the right sidebar so I can start from my actual workday.
user_stories.product_decision: The right sidebar should be a calendar workbench, not a passive list.
acceptance_criteria: The sidebar lists meetings for the selected date.
Events include title, start time, attendee count, recurrence type, and meeting link status.
Agenda sync ignores declined, cancelled, all-day, focus time, out of office, working location, and birthday events.
The empty state makes clear whether there are no meetings or Google is not connected.
test_strategy: Playwright verifies agenda loading, date selection, empty states, and synced event detail open.

user_stories.title: Create recurring series from recurring calendar events
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting owner, I want recurring Google Calendar meetings to become one Minutia series so history and open items carry forward.
user_stories.product_decision: Use recurringEventId as the recurring parent identity and originalStartTime as the stable occurrence identity.
acceptance_criteria: Recurring calendar events create or update one meeting_series row.
Each occurrence creates or updates one meetings row.
Rescheduled recurring instances keep a stable link through originalStartTime.
Captured Minutia notes and A/D/R/I/B items are not overwritten by later calendar sync.
test_strategy: Normalization tests cover recurringEventId, originalStartTime, moved instances, and cancelled instances.
API regression verifies no duplicate series or meetings after repeat sync.

user_stories.title: Create ad hoc meetings from one-off calendar events
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting participant, I want one-off calendar meetings to be capturable without creating a recurring cadence by hand.
user_stories.product_decision: Represent ad hoc calendar meetings as ad hoc Minutia series to preserve the existing data model.
acceptance_criteria: Non-recurring calendar events create or update one ad hoc series.
The generated series is clearly labeled as ad hoc in product surfaces.
Users can open the series from the calendar sidebar.
Repeat sync does not duplicate the ad hoc series or meeting.
test_strategy: Agenda regression covers ad hoc event sync and detail routing.

user_stories.title: Keep Minutia-only series first-class
user_stories.user_story: As a self-hosted or offline user, I want to create series outside Google Calendar so Minutia remains useful without a calendar dependency.
user_stories.product_decision: Calendar-linked series are an acceleration path, not the only creation path.
acceptance_criteria: Manual series creation remains available.
Manual series do not require Google OAuth.
Manual series can still host live meetings, notes, issues, decisions, risks, actions, and blockers.
Calendar metadata remains nullable and does not break manual series.
test_strategy: Existing series creation and meeting detail regressions remain green.

user_stories.title: Start synced calendar meeting from event detail
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting participant, I want to start a synced calendar meeting from the sidebar so the external Meet tab and Minutia capture open together.
user_stories.product_decision: The primary action starts Minutia capture and opens the external meeting URL when available.
acceptance_criteria: Opening a calendar event shows event details and a primary start action.
Start meeting opens meetingUrl in a new tab when present.
The current Minutia tab routes to live meeting capture.
The start endpoint is not called more than once for the same click.
test_strategy: Playwright covers popup creation and route transition.

user_stories.title: Show join state when synced meeting is already live
user_stories.user_story: As a participant viewing a synced event, I want the sidebar to say Meeting in progress and Join now when Minutia capture is already live.
user_stories.product_decision: Use the linked Minutia meeting state as the canonical live state.
acceptance_criteria: Linked live events show Meeting in progress.
The primary action says Join now.
Join now opens the external meeting URL when available and routes to the existing live capture.
Join now does not call the calendar start endpoint or create a duplicate meeting.
test_strategy: Playwright regression covers stale agenda status with live linked meeting state.

user_stories.title: Add incremental agenda sync state
user_stories.user_story: As a workspace member, I want Minutia to keep calendar data fresh without repeated full agenda scans so my sidebar is fast and accurate.
user_stories.product_decision: Persist per-user, per-calendar agenda sync state and use updatedMin for the 14-day sidebar window. Full Calendar syncToken support is reserved for a later full-collection or webhook-backed sync path because Google does not allow syncToken with timeMin, timeMax, or orderBy.
acceptance_criteria: Initial sidebar sync performs a full 14-day agenda window refresh and stores sync state.
Later sidebar sync calls events.list with updatedMin and showDeleted=true.
Incremental sidebar sync does not send syncToken, timeMin, timeMax, or orderBy together.
Deleted calendar entries are processed and reflected safely.
Expired or invalid incremental state triggers a controlled full window resync.
Agenda sync state is scoped by user, organization, calendar, and sync mode.
test_strategy: Unit tests cover updatedMin query construction, pagination, deleted event handling, and invalid state reset.
API regression covers first sync followed by incremental sync with no duplicates.

user_stories.title: Add Calendar push notification webhook
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting participant, I want calendar changes to appear in Minutia soon after they happen so I can trust the sidebar before a meeting.
user_stories.product_decision: Use push notifications as a sync wake-up signal, not as the source of event truth.
acceptance_criteria: Backend can create and renew Events watch channels per connected calendar.
Webhook validates channel tokens before scheduling sync.
Webhook returns a successful status quickly and performs sync asynchronously.
Every notification leads to an incremental sync fetch because Google sends no event payload.
A periodic reconciliation job still runs because notification delivery is not guaranteed.
test_strategy: API tests cover valid channel token, invalid channel token, sync message, exists message, and channel renewal.
Security tests verify webhook cannot trigger cross-user sync.
user_stories.backend_boundary: Privileged webhook handling must live in an isolated backend or Supabase Edge Function, not browser code.

user_stories.title: Show calendar sync health and repair actions
user_stories.user_story: As a workspace member, I want to know when calendar sync is stale, missing a scope, or disconnected so I can fix it before a meeting.
user_stories.product_decision: Treat OAuth and sync health as explicit operational states, not silent failures.
acceptance_criteria: Settings shows last successful calendar sync time.
Settings shows missing calendar or directory scope states separately.
Sidebar shows a compact stale-sync indicator when agenda freshness is degraded.
Reconnect flow requests only the missing capability when feasible.
Disconnect and reconnect preserve Minutia-captured meeting history.
test_strategy: Playwright covers connected, stale, missing directory scope, missing calendar scope, and disconnected states.

user_stories.title: Suggest meeting prep from prior series history
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting owner, I want the sidebar to surface open items and prior decisions before I join so I can walk into the meeting prepared.
user_stories.product_decision: Calendar context should activate Minutia history before the meeting starts.
acceptance_criteria: Event detail shows unresolved issues, actions, risks, and blockers from the linked series.
Event detail shows the last meeting's decisions and notes summary.
Prep content is hidden when no linked series exists.
Prep content does not require starting the meeting.
test_strategy: Playwright covers event detail prep for linked recurring series and ad hoc events.

user_stories.title: Map calendar attendees to Minutia participants
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting participant, I want attendee identities to flow into capture and assignment so ownership is not free text.
user_stories.product_decision: Use attendee email as the stable identity key and Directory search as enrichment.
acceptance_criteria: Calendar attendee emails are stored on linked meetings.
Known workspace members are resolved to Minutia users when possible.
Directory matches provide display name, email, photo, and organization when available.
Issue assignee suggestions prioritize attendees before broader directory matches.
External guests remain selectable as email-backed assignees without requiring workspace membership.
test_strategy: Regression covers attendee-prioritized assignee search and external guest fallback.

user_stories.title: Add conflict and attendance intelligence
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting owner, I want Minutia to warn me when key owners may be unavailable so I can adjust follow-up before the meeting.
user_stories.product_decision: Use FreeBusy only for lightweight availability signals, not invasive calendar detail.
acceptance_criteria: Minutia can query free/busy for selected attendees with proper consent and scopes.
Event detail flags conflicts for assignees of open items due in the meeting.
Conflict UI shows availability state without exposing private event details.
Failure to fetch free/busy does not block meeting start.
test_strategy: API tests cover free/busy success, group too big, too many calendars, not found, and internal error handling.

user_stories.title: Expose calendar-linked meeting data for AI agents
user_stories.user_story: As an AI agent, I need stable APIs for upcoming meetings, linked series, open items, and sync health so I can help prepare and follow up without scraping the UI.
user_stories.product_decision: Calendar integration should be MCP-ready and scriptable from day one.
acceptance_criteria: There is an authenticated API for today's agenda with linked Minutia state.
There is an authenticated API for meeting prep context by calendar event or meeting id.
Responses include stable IDs, source system fields, and sync health metadata.
Agent APIs never expose Google tokens or service-role credentials.
The API contract is documented in JSON schema.
test_strategy: Contract tests validate schema stability and authorization boundaries.

user_stories.title: Import Google Meet transcript artifacts after meetings
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting owner, I want Minutia to import the official Google Meet transcript after the meeting so A/D/R/I/B suggestions can be generated from what was said.
user_stories.product_decision: Start with official post-meeting artifacts because they are supported by the Meet REST API.
acceptance_criteria: Minutia can link a Google Meet conference record to a calendar event or meeting URL.
Minutia can list transcript sessions for a conference record when the authenticated user has access.
Minutia can import transcript entries within the retention window.
Transcript-derived A/D/R/I/B suggestions require user approval before creating durable records.
Transcript import clearly indicates when transcripts were not enabled or are not yet generated.
test_strategy: Integration tests mock Meet transcript resources, entries, missing transcript, and retention expiry.
AI extraction tests validate suggestion shape, source span references, and human approval flow.
privacy_requirements: Show consent language before transcript import.
Do not silently import transcripts for guests or external meetings without explicit user action.
Store source transcript metadata separately from generated A/D/R/I/B suggestions.

user_stories.title: Research live Google Meet media capture
user_stories.user_story: As a meeting participant, I want live A/D/R/I/B suggestions during a Google Meet call so capture happens while the discussion is fresh.
user_stories.product_decision: Treat live capture as a gated research path because official Meet Media API access is Developer Preview.
acceptance_criteria: Document whether the Minutia Google Cloud project can enroll in the Google Workspace Developer Preview Program.
Document OAuth, participant enrollment, admin consent, and disclosure requirements.
Prototype only in a clearly marked experimental path.
Do not ship live media capture to general users until API availability, consent, retention, and security boundaries are production-ready.
If preview access is unavailable, keep Minutia live capture manual and use post-meeting transcript import for automation.
test_strategy: Research spike documents API availability, consent flow, security model, and failure modes.
No production code depends on Developer Preview APIs before a product gate.

success_metrics.definition: Percentage of active users who connect Google Workspace.
Percentage of sidebar calendar events where users open Minutia capture.
Number of duplicate Minutia meetings created per 100 synced calendar starts. Target is zero.
Reduction in manual series setup for meetings that already exist on Google Calendar.
Percentage of A/D/R/I/B items assigned to directory-backed people instead of free text.
Percentage of transcript-derived A/D/R/I/B suggestions accepted by users.

risk_register.impact: Users may be blocked from full Workspace features if Google app verification or admin policy is incomplete.
risk_register.mitigation: Keep scope requests minimal, separate calendar and directory health states, and document admin setup.

risk_register.impact: Sidebar can show stale events or outdated linked meeting state.
risk_register.mitigation: Use sync tokens, push notifications, periodic reconciliation, and explicit sync health states.

risk_register.impact: Moved or cancelled instances can create duplicate or stale meetings.
risk_register.mitigation: Use recurringEventId plus originalStartTime for identity and preserve captured meeting history on destructive calendar changes.

risk_register.impact: Transcript import can violate user expectations if hidden or automatic.
risk_register.mitigation: Require explicit consent, clear source labels, and human approval before generated A/D/R/I/B becomes durable.

risk_register.impact: Live Meet media capture may not be available for general production use.
risk_register.mitigation: Keep live media capture as research and prioritize official post-meeting transcript artifact import first.
